task_completion.description: Open the Sea Level Pressure dataset in NASA Panoply.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The NASA Panoply application window is open with the 'slp.mon.ltm.nc' dataset loaded in the sources/datasets panel.

task_completion.description: Create a plot for the Sea Level Pressure (slp) variable.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A map plot window is open in Panoply displaying the 'slp' (Sea Level Pressure) variable, showing a global or regional map with color-coded pressure data.

task_completion.description: Adjust the plot settings to display data for September.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The time/date array dropdown or label in the Panoply plot window is set to 'September' or the corresponding time index for September.

task_completion.description: Export the September analysis map as a PNG file to the SailRouting directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog or file manager showing 'atlantic_slp_september.png' being saved to or present in the '~/Documents/SailRouting/' directory.

task_completion.description: Create the structured doldrums crossing report in the SailRouting directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor window open showing the doldrums crossing report being written, or a file manager showing 'doldrums_crossing_report.txt' present in the '~/Documents/SailRouting/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the NASA Panoply GUI to generate the map.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Panoply graphical interface being interacted with (e.g., clicking menus, adjusting plot settings), rather than terminal commands executing a plotting script.

integrity.description: The exported map is a genuine output from Panoply.
integrity.visual_evidence: The exported PNG matches the visual style of a Panoply plot (e.g., specific color scales, map projection, title, and legends) and is not a generic downloaded image.

integrity.description: The agent manually created the report based on the Panoply data.
integrity.visual_evidence: A text editor (like gedit, nano, or mousepad) is visible with the report being typed or edited, containing specific latitude/pressure values derived from the map.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target dataset is located at '/home/ga/PanoplyData/slp.mon.ltm.nc' and the target variable is 'slp' (Sea Level Pressure). The expected outputs must be saved in '/home/ga/Documents/SailRouting/' as 'atlantic_slp_september.png' and 'doldrums_crossing_report.txt'. In September, the doldrums (equatorial trough) in the Atlantic are climatologically located between 3°N and 12°N latitude, with minimum pressures around 1013 hPa.
